mission.title: clickhouse: Support for non-scalar values in WITH clause
mission.description: Support for non-scalar values in WITH clause. Requested by 36+ users.

steps.title: Check current clickhouse setup
steps.description:
Verify your clickhouse version and configuration:
```bash
clickhouse-client --version
```
This feature requires a working clickhouse installation.

steps.title: Review clickhouse configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant clickhouse configuration:
Will ClickHouse support WITH clause (common table expression, cte)? Like https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.1/static/queries-with.html

The thing that with CTE I can define temp dataset I can then query multiple times in one query statement.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for non-scalar values in WITH clause
steps.description:
I hereby agree to the terms of the CLA available at: https://yandex.ru/legal/cla/?lang=en

Changelog category (leave one):
- New Feature

Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes to CHANGELOG.md):

Now we support `WITH <identifier> AS (subquery) ... ` to introduce
```yaml
WITH 
  [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9] as numbers,
  arrayMap(x -> x*2, numbers) as doubled_numbers,
  arrayMap(x -> x*x, numbers) as squared_numbers,
  arrayMap( (x,y) -> x+y, doubled_numbers, squared_numbers) as summed_numbers
SELECT
   summed_numbers;
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support for non-scalar values in WITH clause" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I hereby agree to the terms of the CLA available at: https://yandex.ru/legal/cla/?lang=en

Changelog category (leave one):
- New Feature

Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes to CHANGELOG.md):

Now we support `WITH <identifier> AS (subquery) ... ` to introduce named subqueries in the query context. This closes #2416. This closes #4967.
codeSnippets:
WITH 
  [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9] as numbers,
  arrayMap(x -> x*2, numbers) as doubled_numbers,
  arrayMap(x -> x*x, numbers) as squared_numbers,
  arrayMap( (x,y) -> x+y, doubled_numbers, squared_numbers) as summed_numbers
SELECT
   summed_numbers;

prerequisites.description: A working clickhouse installation or development environment.
